tldr: Move back from the Lucide fork to its upstream Feather Icons — a smaller, more minimal set that may be appropriate for legacy projects but loses Lucide's active maintenance and broader catalog.
why_switch: Stay aligned with an existing Feather-based design system
Smaller catalog (~280 icons) enforces visual restraint
MIT license with no behavioral changes from the original Feather aesthetic
Removes Lucide-only icons that don't match a strict minimal style
Simpler API surface for teams that don't need framework-specific packages
what_to_export: List of every Lucide icon name used in the codebase
Wrapper components or hooks that import Lucide icons
Design tokens mapping semantic names to Lucide components
Icon size, stroke-width, and color conventions
Accessibility props (aria-label, role) on icon usages
Catalog of Lucide-only icons not present in Feather
export_method: Grep your codebase for Lucide imports (e.g. from 'lucide-react', 'lucide-vue-next') and produce a unique list of icon names. Cross-reference each name against the official Feather catalog — Lucide is a superset, so many names overlap, but Feather has ~280 icons vs Lucide's 1000+, so some will need substitutes or custom SVGs.
import_method: Install Feather Icons via its official package per the docs (e.g. feather-icons or react-feather per project). Replace Lucide imports with the equivalent Feather component, keeping size/stroke conventions. For icons that exist in Lucide but not in Feather, either pick the closest Feather icon, ship a custom SVG, or keep that surface on Lucide alongside Feather temporarily.

concept_mapping.note: Confirm package name in Feather docs before installing.
Coverage is thinner; consult Feather docs for current options.
Both inherit from the underlying SVG.

concept_mapping.target: Custom SVG or closest Feather match

concept_mapping.note: Both permissive; verify with your legal/compliance process.

gotchas: Feather has ~280 icons vs Lucide's 1000+ — expect missing icons
Feather maintenance is slow; you may inherit unfixed bugs
Framework wrappers for Feather are less polished than Lucide's first-party packages
Switching back loses Lucide-exclusive icons added since the fork
Stroke-width and visual weight should match your existing Lucide usage
Consider whether this move is actually worth it vs staying on Lucide

email.subject: Considering a move from Lucide back to Feather Icons
email.body:
Hey team,

I'm scoping a swap from Lucide to its upstream Feather Icons. The motivation is mostly alignment with an existing Feather-based design system / minimalism, not technical superiority — Lucide is the more actively maintained fork.

Plan: inventory every Lucide icon name in use, cross-reference Feather's ~280-icon catalog, and flag any Lucide-only icons we'd need to replace with custom SVGs or closest matches. Estimated effort: 2-6 hours, plus design review.

Before I start, want to confirm we're okay with the smaller catalog and slower upstream maintenance. Pushback welcome.

Thanks
